articles.title: Americans are feeling a little better about the economy as gas prices ease
articles.description: By Elisabeth Buchwald, Lucy Bayly, CNN (CNN) — After months of souring views driven in part by a surge in gas prices, Americans are feeling more and more optimistic about the economy, according to new data released Friday. Consumer sentiment rose this month from an earlier reading of 48.9 to a final reading of 49.5,

articles.title: Goldman Sachs hikes India’s GDP growth forecast
articles.description: Global investment and financial services firm Goldman Sachs has raised its calendar year 2026 real GDP growth forecast for India by 30 basis points to 6.8% and FY27 forecast by 40 bps to 6.5% in the wake of the fall in crude oil prices.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs hikes India’s GDP growth forecast
articles.description: The firm said lower crude oil prices have also been accompanied by a decline in petrochemical product prices.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia Set to Slash Oil Prices as Hormuz Reopens
articles.description: Saudi Arabia is set to slash August crude prices for Asia by up to $8 a barrel as the Strait of Hormuz reopens and Gulf supply rebounds.

articles.title: Wall Street follows global markets lower as traders sell to lock in profits after recent AI rallies
articles.description: Wall Street pointed toward losses before the opening bell as volatility continued for chipmakers and other companies heavily invested in artificial intelligence. Futures for the S&P 500 lost 0.6% early Friday, while futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average edged 0.2% lower. Nasdaq futures tumbled another 1.4% and are on track for a more than 5% loss this week. In premarket trading Friday, Micron fell 5%, Intel was down 3.6% and Broadcom and Qualcomm both retreated close to 2%. Oil prices drifted even closer to their levels leading up to the U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran. U.S. benchmark crude oil lost $1.62 to $70.30 a barrel.
